commands.repair_reason: Protected maintainer-labeled stacked PR needs human parent-contract and merge-sequencing review; there is no narrow automated code repair to queue.; Cleared: No concrete security or supply-chain regression found; the PR reuses the existing local scripts/ui.js build execution path and does not add dependencies, workflow permissions, or secret handling. (sha=65ae288b55f4a73e4cb24ccb8e4098afb5d8a2c4)
commands.review_summary:
This PR extracts Control UI asset freshness detection/repair helpers, registers an internal core Doctor health check, and routes it positionally through the Doctor contribution runner with focused tests.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a bug reproduction; this is a PR restructuring an existing Doctor repair path. The PR body provides current-head CLI proof for missing Control UI assets with lint/fix/lint behavior.
commands.review_followup: Protected maintainer-labeled stacked PR needs human parent-contract and merge-sequencing review; there is no narrow automated code repair to queue.

target.title: Doctor: structure UI freshness repair
target.body:
## Summary

- Replaces the old broad PR2 with a small non-plugin doctor slice stacked on #85566.
- Extracts Control UI freshness detection/repair helpers from the legacy doctor UI check.
- Registers an internal core health check for missing/stale Control UI assets and runs it positionally through the doctor contribution list.
- Keeps UI freshness out of the broad structured repair pass so the existing doctor order is preserved and the repair is not run twice.
- Preserves the legacy repair-mode UI finding note before the build/rebuild result, and keeps plugin, gateway service, session, policy, SDK, and public health contract surfaces untouched.

## Verification

- git diff --check
- node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts
- node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts
- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts --reporter=dot: 1 file / 26 tests passed
- previous full slice proof on this branch: oxfmt/oxlint touched doctor files, core and core-test tsgo, focused Vitest 4 files / 45 tests, Codex review clean
- codex review --uncommitted: no discrete regression in the UI repair-context fix

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Control UI freshness doctor repair now has structured findings and repair results for missing/stale UI assets while preserving the legacy note/prompt behavior.
Real environment tested: WSL Ubuntu 24.04, source checkout CLI, disposable HOME with isolated OpenClaw state dir.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: With Control UI assets absent in the source checkout, ran `openclaw doctor --lint --only core/doctor/ui-protocol-freshness --json`, then `openclaw doctor --fix --non-interactive --yes --no-workspace-suggestions`, then `openclaw doctor --lint --only core/doctor/ui-protocol-freshness --json` again through the PR source checkout.
Evidence after fix: Before fix, lint returned `ok:false` with one `core/doctor/ui-protocol-freshness` warning: `- Control UI assets are missing. - Run: pnpm ui:build`. During fix, doctor printed the preserved finding note `Control UI assets are missing.`, then printed `UI build complete.`, then completed. After fix, lint returned `ok:true` with `findings:[]`.
Observed result after fix: UI freshness is registered as a core health check, excluded from the broad structured repair pass, run at its own doctor contribution position, preserves the missing-asset note in repair mode, builds the Control UI assets, and the same lint selector is clean after repair.
What was not tested: Full repo check in this rerun; live mutation of a real user profile or production install. The focused tests cover stale/missing asset detection, positional repair routing, broad repair exclusion, and the preserved repair-mode finding note.
target.author: giodl73-repo

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
